content.problemStatement:
The Chef has been kidnapped by aliens ( they want to utilize his excellent cooking skills :P ) and has been transported to the alien planet "ZX532". Interestingly, "ZX532" uses a different time scale which has Y AlienHours on the face of a clock (instead of the 12 hours that we have).  They also have 'X*Y' AlienMinutes (instead of the 60 that we have).  X is defined as the number of minute divisions between each hour (the earth clock has X=5).

The Chef has been forced to cook a lot of dishes by the aliens. He is short of ingredients and hence needs to go out to the market to get them. However when he returns, he makes an interesting observation. The hands of AlienClock have exactly swapped position from the time he left (the big hand and the little hand have switched)!

Given the times between which The Chef might have left and the times between which The Chef may have returned, you are supposed to find out the actual time when he left.  If there is more than one possibility, print the one that maximizes the amount of time that The Chef is out.

**Details of the Clock**

The AlienClock is circular and has exactly 2 hands : the hour hand and the minute hand. The whole circumference of the AlienClock is divided into Y divisions , each of which is further divided into X subdivisions [ X and Y are both positive integers ]. The clock is not discrete but continuous (the hands move continuously instead of moving at certain intervals such as every second, etc.... ).
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two space-separated integers X and Y, where X is the number of minute divisions between each hour and Y is the number of hours on the AlienClock. The second line contains eight space-separated integers a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, representing the time intervals when the Chef might have left (a:b to c:d) and when the Chef may have returned (e:f to g:h).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the time when the Chef left the kitchen in the format 'h:m', where h is an integer and m is rounded to 2 decimal places. If there is no solution possible, output -1 for that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has a solution where the Chef leaves at 1:20.56 and returns at 4:20.56. The second test case has no solution. The third test case has a solution where the Chef leaves at 3:26.43 and returns at 5:26.43.
The Chef leaves at 11:30.00 and returns at 23:30.00.
There is no solution for this test case.

solution.approach: The problem involves finding the time when the Chef left the kitchen given the time intervals when the Chef might have left and when the Chef may have returned. The AlienClock has Y hours and X*Y minutes, and the hands of the clock have exactly swapped positions from the time the Chef left. We can iterate over all possible times when the Chef might have left and check if the swapped time falls within the given return time interval. If it does, we update the maximum time the Chef could have left.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * Y * X * Y) per test case, O(T * Y * X * Y) total.
